channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!! — 59 replies — 147.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!!

Open thread • Replies: 59 • Updated: 2026-06-26 09:05 UTC

OP

>Today, Chainlink, FairSquareLab, a first-mover in core infrastructure within the Korean digital asset ecosystem, UniKA (Unified Korea Alliance), which represents the Korean coalition behind Project Pangea and is comprised of a steering committee of 5 entities (Shinhan Bank, JB Bank, Kbank, FairSquareLab, and OBDIA) along with 10+ participating Korean commercial banks, and Qivalis, a euro stablecoin consortium powered by 37 leading European banks, announced Project Pangea, a strategic, collaborative task force focused on redefining global foreign exchange markets by unlocking real-time, stablecoin-based cross-border settlement models.


https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/chainlink-and-multinational-banking-consortia-launch-project-pangea-to-develop-t0-settlement-framework-for-international-fx-markets-302807910.html

Early replies (first 3)

62401710
Wow I can’t wait for this to not mean anything.

62401722
p+c etc.
don't care until the needle moves on this FUCKING GODDAMN TOKEN

62401738
>>62401704
xrp sneeded?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62409639
>>62409633
Why do you guys keep calling me gay? I mean, I am bi but what's wrong with that? I am one of you! We should be able to band together Voltron this shit! LINK doesn't need us to for it to thrive but we might as well be in harmony as our investment carries us hand in hand to the moon!

62409640
>>62409606
>>62409613

62409661
>>62409640
How i feel in this thread! Meanwhile, I got the picture I am attaching to this comment in my back pocket, (which none of them know) just waiting to pull it out like the ultimate trump card. (¬‿ ͡¬) Dare I?

62409666
>>62406112
I don't know, but I sipped more link

62409669
>>62409666
>>62409583

62409675
>mB3FqHQS

62409679
>>62409675
Who is this? He looks crazy and scared. Is this from a movie or something? I don't recognize it. TL;DR?

62409681
>>62409675
I just saw you named the picture xrp. Are you saying this is XRP realizing the severity of what I posted in "62409661?" If so, post it again, because you are surely right!

62409739
i don't care anymore. they could do away with the token and send it to 0 and I would just post a LINK and picrel crossover thread to laugh at the copers who will be forever employed and get on with the rest of my life as if nothing happened and i didn't spend thkusands of hours reseaeching bullshit and making rarely reposted link memes for 7 years.
fuck you saturn

62409752
>>62409661
bless the undeserving, king

62409768
>>62409739
do things like that really happen though? No watchers, no justice? the whole token just poof?

62409835
>>62409752
*drops shades revealing the same knowing look from before, but sharper, and slams the picture down on table* BOOOOOM!

62409844
>>62409835

62409848
>>62409844
Exactly!

62409884
>>62409768
the token doesn't need monetary value to serve its technical purposes, you can incentivize everyone with other assets, staking has obviously been unnecessary to secure value etc. it's "happened" in all but name. but it might work out anyway, idk.

62409907
price

62410035
>>62409884
>the token doesn't need monetary value to serve its technical purposes
Same is true for Bitcoin, ETH, ...
Bitcoin was arguably way more secure when it was literal cents, and random faggots ran full mining nodes on their home PCs.

62410246
>mB3FqHQS
I've said it before and I'll say it again: the BOOOOOM meme absolutely destroyed some xerpies on here. Can't believe that after all the abuse and bullying they took for 10 years, simply posting the catchphrase of one of their twitter schizos is what did them in. Praying for you crazy bastards.

62411245
>>62410035
>duuuude it's not fair, bitcoin did xyz...

so how does it work in your head? 40 million altcoins should all have bitcoin or ethereum type price runup so things can become... fair?

you see folks, this is how the logic of a chainlink boyscout operates. no understanding that there isn't enough liquidity for their special altcoin to run like early crypto's did back when there was only like 50 of them. no understanding that obviously they aren't early anymore and therefore the days of those speculative runs are over.

instead of accepting the market they have, they cry like a child complaining to their parents that the other kids were allowed to have cookies before dinner.

they are: the few, the retarded... THE Cuckolds of crypto

62411677
>>62410035
i don't think the same is true for btc and eth. their ongoing function and adoption (by miners) depended on value realizable by the token. Link apparently doesn't have the same restriction.

62411795
>>62411677
>i don't think the same is true for btc and eth
Not only is what I said true for btc and eth, but both operated beautifully for a long time at a value of literally cents, proving that what I said is true for btc and eth.

>>62411245
>BTC, ETH, SOL, BNB, XRP, ADA, TRX, Link, ... don't need monetary value to serve its technical purposes
>but ONLY Link cannot pump because of this reason

How does that work in your head?

62413787
>>62411795
at no point were eth or eth block rewards worth cents. btc, fair enough point, but you will concede that further adoption and miner buildout since that brief time has happened almost exclusively because of future price.
the same is not true for link, where price isn't the product or even a feature of its ongoing adoption.

62413804
>>62410246
I don't understand. How did the meme destroy them? I thought it was just us doing a sort of thing like the Costco Guys thing to have a sort of war cry and celebration chant over our success when news came out about link? That is the only context I have seen the meme so far here.

62413805
>>62401710
The future is now

62414082
>>62413805
What if we've always been living in the future as alien avatars and we traveled back in time to give Chainlink to ourselves (people say this about the small grey aliens a lot that they are like bio robots controlled remotely, what if they were implanted with our incarnated spirits?) and so the future was always? Brain BLOWN!

62415086
>>62413787
>at no point were eth or eth block rewards worth cents
lmao, this is explicitly about the price of the token/coin, not the value of mining rewards.

>further adoption and miner buildout since that brief time has happened almost exclusively because of future price
>link, where price isn't the product or even a feature of its ongoing adoption
We were talking about the "technical purposes" of the blockchain.
The technical purposes of Bitcoin and ETH were fully met when the price of both was merely cents.

You're talking about speculation, which is the same for any crypto.

62415177
>i4/u3PPu
>h/d99FjL
>the moronic shill who accuses everyone for samefagging is a samefag himself
ironic
notice how not one shill will even mention the AMA

62415187
>>62415177
>the AMA
humiliation ritual
>Hey guys, we know you've got a lot of burning questions about the project so we're hosting an AMA for you
>*top questions are about staking, tokenomics, and the function of the Reserve*
>ok, now that you've tuned in I will proceed to tell you about my favorite dApps and my opinion on using crypto in restaurants
>well, time's up now... thanks for tuning in!

62415313
>>62415086
ah i see you do not want to talk about the role of price and future value in sustaining the "technical purpose" of the network because nascent BTC was briefly worth pennies. also eth at no point traded for less than a few dollars once the network was producing blocks.

62415322
>>62415313
The price of ETH or BTC has pretty much no bearing on the technical purpose of the network. See the fact that both networks worked perfectly fine when the price was extremely low.
You're thinking of speculation.

item.title: ITT WE LAUGH AT SILVERNIGGERS AHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAH… — 69 replies — 47.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
ITT WE LAUGH AT SILVERNIGGERS AHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAHAAHAHAHAHAHAH…

Open thread • Replies: 69 • Updated: 2026-06-26 08:02 UTC

OP

ITT WE LAUGH AT SILVERNIGGERS

62406474
>>62406449
Not selling.

62406483
>>62406474

62406488
>>62406474
Quiet jamboy. The colonizers are laughing at your "investment".

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62408419
>>62407950
kek cryptards are getting brain damage from being fucked so hard

62408483
>>62406449
i sold at the top

62408988
>>62406449
Didn't buy the top. Still in profit. Not selling.

62409027
>>62406449
I do love silver getting into slurping range

62409062
They bought silver because a rich indian proved its a scam,,,,,,lmao,,,,,,pol brain and investing is a deadly combination

62409141
>>62408105
lol N****r

62409263
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOM SO BULLISH AT SILVER
SUPPLY SHOCK WHEN????

62409270
>>62409263
Theres a 54 billion ounce glut of silver

62409295
>>62409263
>SUPPLY SHOCK WHEN
Comex and Shanghai exchange inventories have been going up now for a solid month. Industry is thrifting silver and replacing it as much as possible with copper and in silver just being a rock it has no economic value really compared to gold.

62409326
>>62406449
What retard didn't sell once that shit shot past $100?

62409335
>>62409326
The ones spamming the catalog about how smart they were

62409381
>>62409326
Stackturds all claim silver is going to $2000.....bunch of losers

62409428
>>62406449
Just count the minutes before the next global crisis and precious metals go back up

62409431
>>62409428
Like the iran war and silver plummeted....lmao nothing dumber or broker than a stacktard

62409935
>>62409428
>global crisis
>silver just dumps
LMAO

62409962
>>62409428
Silver and gold dump dring a crisis loljust look at how they dumped harder than the stock market during the financial crisis.

62410278
>>62406587
Hodl silver hands

62410350
>>62406501
so why not just donate the money to charity then if you can never sell ?

62410373
>>62409326

t.stacktard

62410390
I'm still up on silver from inheriting my dad's coins a few months ago.

62410451

Still up 3x

62410696
>>62410451
>Bought a monster box in 2019 and another 200 oz over 2020-2021
>Still up 3x
factually incorrect, at best you are up 2x in 7 years, not counting inflation
lol

62410726
>>62406474
HODL FELLOW APES
DIAMOND HANDS
TO DA MOOOOOOON!

62410762
silver will never lose it's true value unlike your stocks and shitcoins

62411945
>>62410762
what value, that you can put it in your asshole?

62412080
>>62407084
Imagine shitting silver.

62412276
Oh no, my 10oz bar I paid $130 for is now only worth $580, I’m so sad.

62412388
>>62406474
SHINY ROCKS

62415167

62415205

item.title: Polkadot update — 35 replies — 18.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot update

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-06-26 07:37 UTC

OP

ITS HERE

XRP, ETHEREUM AND LINK IS COOKED

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

https://news.openshore.io/web3-summit-2026-recap.html

Early replies (first 3)

62399118
5. Privacy is a Competitive Advantage
In a world of surveillance capitalism, Polkadot's emphasis on privacy-by-default and user agency differentiates it sharply from other ecosystems. The proof-of-personhood protocol and private payment demonstrations showed concrete technical approaches, not just rhetoric.

Do they think big Google and Facebook will let them get away with this?

62399184
After further research

It seems we have a fork of consensus

https://forum.polkadot.network/t/introducing-a-new-jam-token/13029

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

So what the fuck is JAM and how will it bring value back to DOT marketcap?

62399190
>The JAM protocol presupposes the existence of a token to ensure soundness in its state usage. I argue that DOT would make a highly suboptimal choice and that a new, fixed-issuance token to represent the new Polkadot resource of JAM state makes the most sense. Assuming DOT DAO were to launch a Polkadot JAM for its upgrade, then this new resource-access token would be wholly owned by DOT DAO by default. DOT utility would continue as at present, implying control of DOT DAO — and with it, effective ownership of the Polkadot JAM — as well as the means for staking, collateral and coretime payment within the Polkadot ecosystem.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62399395
>>62399197

Linkie crying seeing another crypto vibing

62400350
>>62399184
a new resource is being introduced to Polkadot with the JAM upgrade (fast shared storage like RAM) and you need one JAMKB per KB of RAM you want to use on the network, so you can't buy more tokens than there are KBs of RAM

62400419
>>62399084
DOT will make us all rich beyond our Wildest dreams!!

62401678
>>62399084
I'll believe they're serious when I see this on an fdroid repository

62401689
>>62401678
they had a grapheneOS build for the event so it is likely that will happen

62401700
>>62399197
Cope

62401739
>>62401689
Interesting
Just bought a few k
We'll see how this goes

62401877
>>62401739
A lambo will be in your future

62401946
>>62401877
Checked.

62402307
>>62399251
You have a missed call

62402326
>>62402317
Didn't NEO have a similar issue

62402419
>>62402317
it's just an nft with price discovery so they don't have to do all of that coretime auction bullshit again

62403350
>>62402317

Thats not even what its use is for

62403448
>>62402317

im surprised dot isnt trading at 10 dollars considering the pedigree

but they overspent on marketing during a bear market instead of 2021. but on the plus side the dot treasury has started reporting quarterly profits. Unlike Cardano or Chainlink

it wont stay under a dollar for long

62404229
>>62403448
>it wont stay under a dollar for long

Under 50 cents by 2027

62404438
0.87 new low lmao. god what a piece of shit.

62404588
15k @ 7$.
I dont care

62404599
>>62404229
It'll go to 0. Nobody cares; it's a useless chain that serves to inflate Gayvin's ego, making him believe he's intelligent and respected. This desperate propaganda on /biz/ shows how dead this project is.

62405376
>>62401877
Checked
I've heard that before
We'll see
Been waiting for an actual parallel economy system for fucking ever
Was watching komodo and loki/oxen before this and they never got off the ground

62405404
Depression

62405433
Didn't read but is he hugging DOT and shilling a new token?

62406887
I bought more

62408015
>>62406887
Rookie move

62408521
>>62404588

If you buy 15k more your average will be 3.50

Why not DCA?

62408874
>>62404438

.885

62411243
wowie

62413316
>>62405433

JAM will not be a new token but will be an on chain platform with Polkadotbl

item.title: >see people saying "2.5% are liquid millionaires" >leave out the part where a huge chunk are retirement-age investors wh… — 21 replies — 17.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
>see people saying "2.5% are liquid millionaires" >leave out the part where a huge chunk are retirement-age investors wh…

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-06-26 08:16 UTC

OP

>see people saying "2.5% are liquid millionaires"
>leave out the part where a huge chunk are retirement-age investors who had 40+ years of compounding
>bought blue chips when they were practically giving them away
>fast forward to today
>if you're under 50 the percentage is probably way lower
>it id 0.2%
>that is less than 1 in 40 oma
>look around the next time you're in a grocery store
>9.9 out of 10 people aren't sitting on $1M in liquid investments
>the guy with seven figures isn't standing in line with you
>everyone else is arguing over 5% raises and coupon apps
>the wealth gap isn't a gap
>it's a different universe


Why will YOU be in the top 0.2%?

Early replies (first 3)

62412965
>>62412950
Because I hate niggers and jews, but I'm good at playing the jew-game. God has chosen me to be the chosen one, the one who will fund antisemite institutions and finally make the final solution real, as the Lord sad it will once come to play with the real Messiah. Amen.

62413039
>>62412950
I thought the same way when I was 20. But I invested anyway, and now I'm one of the liquid millionaires with $2.6m liquid. I stand in line at the grocery store like everybody else. If you want to become rich then sitting around on your ass bitching about how unfair life is because nobody is handing you million dollar checks isn't the way to do it.

62413053
>>62412950
>bought blue chips when they were practically giving them away
Actually at the time I was pissed at how expensive they were. You want to buy blue chips then hop to it asap, they have a tendency to get more expensive over time.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62413065
>>62412950
I’m 35 and my NW is 140k.

Will booglehead tier investing get me to “a little better than average” if I’m dumping 2kish a month in my brokerage and maxing my Roth IRA yearly?

62413067
>>62412950
The wealth gap is natural.

62413069
>>62412950

Just buy DOT lol

https://polkadot.cloud/blog/en/jam-12-to-20-months-m1

62413075
>>62413039
Not really, you got lucky. 2.5% of people have 1m at best. How old are you now?
>>62413039
>>62413053
Income is getting fucked now so people don’t have much to invest. It is savings first then investments. People don’t have savings.

62413088
I'm not going to sit in a million bucks worth of SGOV just to say I'm a liquid millionaire.

62413103
>>62413088
1m net worth is still 10% of Americans and most of it is tied up in primary home.

62413143
>>62413075
I got lucky by investing instead of sitting around whining about not being rich and gaslighting myself into thinking it was impossible. Stop being such a useless whiny little bitch and work on improving your finances

62413167
>>62413143
But you’re old now.

62413200
>>62413167
Nope. I just put in the work to make myself wealthy, carefully evaluating stocks and taking risks that I thought were reasonable. Nobody is going to walk by and just toss you a couple mil just for the lulz. If you want to become rich, it's up to you to get yourself there.

62413214
>>62413200
Yeah you got lucky lol.

62413325
>>62413214
Working hard and investing intelligently isn't luck no matter how many times you say it. If you want to sit on your ass and whine like a bitch and be poor, then have at it. But don't lie to me and everybody else about how "unlucky" you are while you're at it .

62413354
>>62412950
Having passive money is like playing on god mode

62413357
>>62413325
how old are you? can you comprehend basic questions?

62413365
>>62413357
Ask more politely next time.

62413388
>>62413365
You’re stupid and got lucky. The stats show that.

62415048
>>62412950
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

62415141
This chart doesnt realize that the "productivity" is just fake money printing bullshit. We're actually losing productivity.

62415222
>>62412950
My parents are millionaires and are retired, they've always been very frugal. Growing up my dad drove the same piece of shit Volvo Stationwagon that had like 300k miles because he was too cheap to replace it. They sold their house a few years back and downsized to a new one for $800k, paid in cash. It's already appreciated like 10%.

My dad almost flunked out of college but graduated with a computer engineering degree in the 70's and got a job at IBM when they were hiring anyone with a pulse who knew how to turn on a PC, he's been getting a pension from IBM for like 10 years despite the fact he quit working there back in the 90's. He cashed out his 401k and invested in a boomer money market fund that doesn't even beat inflation because he's convinced the economy is about to crash. They also get Social Security and Medicare which probably won't exist when I retire.

item.title: 1 BTC = 1 BTC — 11 replies — 8.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
1 BTC = 1 BTC

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-06-26 08:13 UTC

OP

1 BTC = 1 BTC

Early replies (first 3)

62412737
1 BTC = 1000 Silver

62412801
>>62412603
thats someones son

62412943
>>62412603
>1 BTC = 1 BTC

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62412945
>>62412801
That's how he turned out this way

62413400
>>62413297
>posted it again
>commodities chad lol

62413490
>>62412603
I bagged my cousin a time or two nun wrong with it

62413493
1000 btc= 5 grams of cupronickel occasionally 6 grams
5oz of silver= 1 new minivan
5oz of gold = one 3bdrm house in soCal
Sorry not sorry thats just the way its going 2 be from here on out.

62413503
>>62412603
nothing is illegal if you dont get caught

62415116
>>62412801
obviously an extremely beautiful woman

62415219
>>62413490
Sweet home Alabama

item.title: Who uses Polkadot? — 9 replies — 7.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Who uses Polkadot?

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-06-26 08:13 UTC

OP

With the Cardano dApp ecosystem completely imploding from the TapTools closure and the SecondFi exploit, it’s completely obvious Substrate is the only architectural survivor.

Cardano-cels thought Haskell was safe, but asynchronous backing and Polkadot 2.0 Agile Coretime prove that modular shared security is the only way to scale without draining user wallets. Now that the DOT treasury is finally pivoting away from multi-million dollar logo rebranding and actually funding core developer infrastructure, the ecosystem metrics are completely undeniable.

Why aren't you loading up on DOT at these prices before the parachain coretime marketplace goes fully liquid? It's literally a mathematically guaranteed breakout.

Early replies (first 3)

62412459
>>62412453

I do use Polkadot everyday

Its on my autobuy

62412625
>>62412453
awaiting the next bridge exploit to completely drain the polkadot system yet again

62412713
>>62412625

This is impossible

Polkadot is one of the most secure cryptos

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62412947
>>62412713
look up what happened to acala and moonbeam, the biggest parachains

62412986
>>62412947

1. Aftermath: Acala recovered relatively well with community/governance intervention. This remains the main notable incident for the project. No major new breaches reported since.

2. No direct security breach: Searches turn up no evidence of a core protocol hack or exploit on Moonbeam itself. It was tangentially involved in the Acala incident (e.g., as a destination for some swapped funds or via shared Polkadot ecosystem elements like XCM channels)

???

62413181
>>62412986
AI is such shit. Acala never fucking recovered, it has a marketcap under 1 mil and legitimately went to zero (picrel). At the same time you had Moonbeam get completely drained by the Nomad bridge hack, with the team insisting they did no wrong despite being responsible for selecting the bridge against the protests of their own community and the bridge being financially backed by people affiliated with the team.

62413320
Nobody cares dude. Security? Scalability? Who gives a shit, all crypto is heading to zero. Agile Coretime and asynchronous who-gives-a-fuck… do you still think it’s 2021? I don’t care how agile the coretime is, it’s all garbage. The sooner you realize that the better off you’ll be.

62415156
50 bucks

62415218
>>62412453
>asynchronous backing and Polkadot 2.0 Agile Coretime prove that modular shared security is the only way to scale without draining user wallets
Make sure you calibrate the plasma conduits and do a full scan of the Jeffries' tubes too.

The dumbest of retards fall for technobabble. You are unworthy of life for being so gullible.

item.title: >Polkadot reached its all-time high market capitalization of approximately $63 billion on November 4, 2021, driven by a… — 6 replies — 3.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
>Polkadot reached its all-time high market capitalization of approximately $63 billion on November 4, 2021, driven by a…

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-06-26 07:35 UTC

OP

>Polkadot reached its all-time high market capitalization of approximately $63 billion on November 4, 2021, driven by a peak token price of $55.13.

>XRP marketcap today = 65 billion

Early replies (first 3)

62412982
>>62412967
Don't let them know what an inflationary asset is. Leave the bagholders alone.

62412996
>>62412967
as far as I know monero lets you make anonymous payments that are untraceable
what can "polkadot" do

62413008
>>62412982
>>62412996

Inflation was fixed in the Spring

Current Situation (as of June 2026)
Hard supply cap: 2.1 billion DOT (implemented via community referendum and runtime upgrade around March 14, 2026).

This replaced the previous uncapped model.

Inflation reduction: Annual new issuance dropped sharply (e.g., from ~120 million DOT to ~56–57 million DOT initially — a ~53% cut). The effective net inflation rate is now around ~3.1% (after burns from treasury, coretime sales, etc.), and it continues to decline.

"Pi Schedule" disinflation: New issuance reduces by ~13.14% of the remaining gap to the cap every two years. This creates a stepped, predictable decline toward near-zero inflation over decades (projected to approach the cap around 2160, with ~1.91B DOT by ~2040).

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62413009
>>62412996
I'm actually hoping it makes me a ton of money to throw into xmr lmao

62413024
>>62412996

Monero is a double edged sword. Europe is going to phase them out next. Some countries have banned it entirely.

The point of crypto isn't being anonymous like some people think, the main point is to hold any other asset other than the global reserve currency (greenbacks)

Polkadot is going to be the first chain to have not fast, but IMMEDIATE resources from JAM

https://medium.com/polkadot-network/dot-dao-and-the-need-for-jamkb-a069e72e9728

62415155
50 dollars

item.title: Why aren't you holding BTC? — 74 replies — 121.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren't you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 74 • Updated: 2026-06-26 08:53 UTC

OP

Too expensive? Prefer alts? Don't trust it? Or do you simply think there are better opportunities elsewhere?
Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62395695
you can get better gains on stocks. those companies work for your money. btc sits there doing nothing but has the same risk profile as sharing a hobos needle without greater fools left to carry it to $200k

62395915
give me reasons to hold btc.

62395925
I am holding because I bought in 2020 before etfs now I refuse to sell because I'll get audit raped by the CRA supposedly so I'm stuck holding until it's got better tax laws or borrowing rates.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62409560
>>62409236
Depends what you mean by risky. I wouldn't touch an unaudited protocol, but I also don't think every new protocol carries the same level of risk.

62409564
>>62399898
Governments taxing something doesn't make it dead. If anything, it usually means they've accepted it's an asset class that's here to stay.

62409803
>>62409560
More like defi hacks

62409813
>>62409107
It's possible 10 years from now. Pass it to your kids

62409997
>>62409803
That's why I'm watching Babylon instead of blindly aping into random protocols. They've been taking audits and testing seriously.

62410003
>>62406014
People have gone broke waiting for the perfect entry just as often as they've bought too early.

62410039
>>62410003
So what's the best time to buy

62410525
>>62410039
everytime, imo.

62411160
>>62410525
I don't have money

62411213
>>62409997
I think their TBVs could unlock a lot of Bitcoin use cases that weren't really possible before. We'll know a lot more once the Aave V4 public testnet is complete tho.

62411224
>>62409051
Pls summary

62411241
>>62396002
What About gold and silver?

62411256
>>62404304
I'm not convinced .

62411265
I will just keep my 0.3 btc somewhere

62411638
>>62395915
Institutions are getting onboard, It might do 10X in a decade, also lending is starting to become more relevant with the likes of TBV, there are so many utilities untapped.

62412797
>>62411160
go to work

62413082
>>62411638
That's the part most people miss. BTC doesn't have to just sit in a wallet forever.

62413193
>>62406783
YOU'RE RETARD

62414853
>>62409813
Good idea

62414893
>>62395655
>Why aren't you holding BTC?
because there is this one nigger saylormoon who hold nearly a million of these, only boughted with debts & baggies hopes and dreams. MSTR alone was most of the buying pressure the past 2 years. What would the price look like to your opinion if it wasn't for Mike buying more than all the BTC mined worldwide each year?
We would already be back in the 15k range if it wasn't for him throwing infinite monies at BTC. And now his preferential dividend model is failing, do you really believe MSTR will be able to continue the purchase frenzy?
In just 6 months STRC went from 7 years worth of dividends to pay in their treasury down to 13 months. This boat is bleeding cash left and right.

62414918
>>62409051
Stolen from Sam Hyde

62414938
>>62414893
He knows what's he's doing

62414950
>>62413082
It won't work forever

62414955
>>62414944
How?

62415214
>>62414950
Hmmm, that's why it's still in public testnet instead of being pushed straight into production. I'm more interested in where it could lead than writing it off this early.

62415217
>>62414893
MSTR has definitely been a major buyer, but I don't think Bitcoin's entire value proposition depends on one company continuing to buy forever.

62415228
>>62395655
Because I have no brown, black or asian admixture.

62415296
>>62395655
Bitcorn has no liquidity now - there are no baggies left to trap.

I'd rather gamble on stocks - at least the price actually moves.

62415297
>>62396879
There is 'demand' for literally meme garbage like PEPE and DOGE which do nothing - not really an argument.

62415301
>>62415217
No, but MSTR collapse will cause a negative doom loop for Bitcorn if they have to start dumping.

item.title: VOO and Chill — 91 replies — 27.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 91 • Updated: 2026-06-26 06:11 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62409479
>ETF and chill is smart but I'm smarter I can time the market!

62409545
>>62400493
honestly everything is fucked. index fund bagholders are forced to buy shitstocks and act like paypiggies for pump and dumpers like elon musk. it's literally causing a bubble. nobody wins except billionaires who force your grandma's retirement fund to pay for their malfunctioning AI.

62409811
>low fee index funds
it's VTI and VXUS for me

62409901
>>62400636
lol doomer, stop spreading negativity. you will be a lot happier even if you just invested 150 a month.

62410053
>>62409901
>Realism is negativity!

62410067
>>62400493
wtf are you doing bringing common sense into my retard and bot zoo?

62410086
>>62400636
>The problem is we have people starting at age 35 with like 150 a month thinking it's going to somehow mean something.
pop quiz! how much would you expect your money to grow over 150 months (12.5 years) with a conservative 6% annual return?

62410375
Yep just bought another share of VOO

62410405
>>62403053
They are also expecting SS and medicare to buff out their income and reduce their costs. Everyone here is likely planning to have no SS or other government pension.

62410414
>>62402995
>>62403044
Really you two are dumb as fuck. Do you not understand what FIRE is?

VOO has 15% CAGR since inception. 11.75% after inflation. With even a generous withdrawal rate you can live very comfortably on it if you have enough capital. My business partner does this, goes on vacations all the time. He has a couple of million invested across UK-equivalent ETFs like VUSA. Loads of people do this, it's not the absolute best use of capital but it's a good return for how liquid and low fee it is.
>>62403044
Learn to read you fucking imbecile. I posted that only millionaires CAN LIVE OFF IT. . Anyone can invest and make gains from ETFs but you won't get rich unless you invest for decades.
>Also getting rich quick is literally the point of all this
The point of what? Investing? No it isn't. The point of ETFs? LOL. You are just some dumb teen shitcoiner with sky high time preference I can just tell. Go and buy more shitcoins so you can "get rich quick" dickhead.

62410968
>>62410414
This is why I just wanna get as much capital in there as soon as possible it just seems like something I can actually control versus chasing returns
Don’t have any ideas though

62410981
>>62407086
how about a job nigger

62411192
>>62410981
I am currently at my full time salary job right now silly

62412068
VOO bros are up bigly today

62413691
>>62412068
sweet

62413705
>>62400493
Felix and friends on youtube has a good way of putting it. Why invest in 500 companies when 50%+ are going down and sideways? If you have the time and money and a good strategy you can stick to you're better off picking stonks. But most people don't have the time, money and discipline to do so.

62413718
>>62413705
I think it's more like 20% of the s&p carries the other 80%, it is definitely lopsided. He (an ex investment banker) preaches buy and hold is a dead strategy.

62413734
>>62410053
Your "realism" doesn't sound realistic to people who actually do this stuff. It sounds retarded, like you've got no idea what you're actually talking about.

62413777
>>62413705
>>62413718
bc i cannot pick the winners, simple, look what happened today

62413963
If you don't just want to do VOO and chill only, just have a separate account at Vanguard that you treat like a savings account and deposit something there monthly to buy VOO or whatever. You'll be glad later on.

62414091
>>62412068
>checking your account daily
>investing only in one country

62414103
For me its 90% VOO
10% GPIQ
So i win even when I crab

62414142
>>62413705
>Felix and friends on youtube
>ex investment banker
>"Discover A Trading System That Makes Market Headlines Irrelevant"

62414162
>>62400493
Fuck VOO
>50% VT
>50% VTI
That's the real chill. Total U.S. investment is 81% of portfolio, you capture emerging markets, and some great companies you otherwise wouldn't have in VOO or VTI alone.

62414279
>>62414162
What’s the best way to compare the two? Track the CAGRs of VOO vs the average of VT and VTI combined

62414680
>>62414279
If you do that, its boomer tier analysis, otherwise that analysis would dictate to just buy VOO or QQQ overlooking the real value of VT/I. VT is used as a hedge if there's an exodus from U.S. markets in the next 5-10 years. VTI is used as a hedge against an exodus from bloated tech in the next 5-10 years but the U.S is still supreme (likely the case). When investment eventually settles from AI bloat and only 3-5 companies out of the current hundreds trying to pursue AI development come out on top, investment will switch into a different sector. With these etfs, you dont have to worry about what the next big thing is, which is why its the ultimate hold. Zero think. Comfy returns and they trail VOO by total return over 10 years 309% vs 325%, VT and VTI both have lowe p/e which makes the more value and less risk. Buy, hold, DRIP, and sell when you want to. This is the long end of my portfolio, and my 401k and IRAs. This is shit I am not touching for 30-40 years. Some people in my firm freak out over market turmoil, im zen, where one bucket empties, another fills for me.

62414768
>>62413705
>Felix and friends
pic is the better Felix

62414776
>>62414162
vti+vxus is more tax efficient

62414829
>>62414776
Barely, but VXUS does have the pas-through tax credit, something that amounts to $100 per $100k invested, which is better, plus you can set your parameters for how much you want allocated to international. But have you ever had to explain to a 24yo HR roastie to set up 15% portfolio allocation of VTIAX/VXUS?
>HALF HERE HALF THERE IT CANT BE THIS FUCKING HARD
The headache is not worth the hassle.

62415002
>>62410414
VOO is not 15%, its 10%
>since inception
I don't give a shit, its such a low sample that it's nearly statistically irrelevant. Here is that data from 1900-2016. US Large is VOO

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 159 replies — 16.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 159 • Updated: 2026-06-25 23:57 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62398876
>>62398868
https://voca.ro/1aI6fGYlNRNN

62398905
>>62394942
Don’t get that either. Even if you only bought full shares how could you not afford 200 dollars worth of shares? Is this under 10k net worth or under 100$ net worth

62399272
>>62398789
>Wavelength (λ) (THE SYMBOL IN HALF LIFE)

Distance between repeating points of a wave (e.g., crest to crest). Determines frequency and, for light, color.

>Amplitude:

Maximum height of the wave. Determines intensity (brightness for light, loudness for sound).

>Phase

Position of a wave within its cycle. Determines how waves add or cancel when combined.

>Polarization

Direction of oscillation of a transverse wave. For light, it is the orientation of the electric field.

62399358
Tomorrow we shall give predictive programming a try as photonics will likely be a difficult venture

62399394
10k- networth reporting in! You guys are too smart for me with all those grand ideas.

62400367
>>62398789
tl;dr

62401110
7 figure club. I already lost more than 10k today at market open. Stay poor fags

62401765
I just wrote a super long post with all sorts of trade basics and an emphasis for this group to define normal position exit conditions so you can tangibly ask anons "did you exit before, during, or after the standardized 4chan-exit signals?" Along with recommending covered calls for slow moving but trend holding markets like gold/BTc/xmr or oil when watching forex markets. 10y+dxy are good to watch too.
Blah blah blah
But this whole thread degenerated into trying to sell "art", and not every anon working together for every group success to contribute to all that participate, but rather instead for every anon to work on their own project, by themselves, but as a group selling the same products/watering down their labor's value.

>>62401110
This is who you should ask for advice. This anon only lost 1% of his shit n that's if he's only got 1m. Not a big deal. Don't you anons want to be able to lose 10k casually n be unbothered yet responsible?

62401809
>>62401765
I did it the hard way. Degree in finance. Work in finance. Almost 20 years. Been investing since the 08 crash. There's no quick trick or easy money. Crypto is bullshit. Just keep buying.

62401893
Six figures is truly hell, I miss being proud of 70k.

62403333
>>62401765
It was one art guy

62403337
>>62401765
This general also is a work in progress, I am hoping 10 generals from now we have a much better work flow, plan and ultimately success. In some capacity.

62404260
Bump

62404574
No point in this general. Fastest way to make 10k is just to work for a bit, you shouldn’t be investing

62404709
Bump

62404737
>>62373752
its a spacex thread?
its time to buy o better buy in few days one week

62405715
bump

62407354
Page 8 bump, /biz/ must be losing money again because there's an uptick in activity.

62407450
almost 9 months since i started my first job after college
this feels stagnant and joyless
what exactly do i need to do to escape wageslavery and poverty

62407931
Someone send me ten grand so I can join the club, thanks

62408558
>>62407450
What were you doing in 2017, 2021 and December of 2024?

62408565
>>62407354
Thank you, my wage job prevents me from dedicating my energies to this thread but I should have some time soon

62408611
>>62408584
Wtf are you on about dude, yes I made 54 posts over the span of 8 days. I'm surprised you're not taking your own advice and leaving 4chan.

62410044
Bump

62410051
Under 10k you just need to have a job and save cash.
You need cash flow and if you dont have shit a job is the only way to start.

62410731
Bump

62411520
>>62408558
>2017
still in high school
>2021
still in uni
>December of 2024
just graduated and was looking for jobs (i still had 0 dollars to my name at that point)

62411614
>>62373752
>>62373772
>>62373798
>>62373799
>>62373803
>post Maccari's "Cicero Denounces Catiline" (1888)
>crop out Catiline
Fuck you OP, I'm glad you're poor.

62412314
>>62411520
You could've played the memecoin casino in 2021.

62413842
Bump, I'll be free tomorrow

62414429

62414447

62414497

62414628

62414808

62414819

nevermind selling investment grade bonds at 4% and making use of SBLOC at 6... saylor will mint 10000000000 billion STRC to pay for STRC yield... because... idk

62414840
>>62414616

62414845

62414848

62414850

62414871

62414969

OP

So many stablecoins are rugging right now it's crazy

62408562

62408566

62408598
>>62408557

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62408623

62408804
>>62408566

62408875
>>62408804
no it isn't, this happened today

62409989
>>62408557

62410014
>>62408557
Wow so stable

62411316
would pay $100 to watch an interview between caleb hammer and the largest magic internet money bagholder

62411344
>>62408557
That's when you know the bear is getting serious and there will be major dumps until we hit the bottom.

62414655
>>62408557
Last time I farmed this was almost a year ago, the APY would frequently go to zero and they would have to fucking manually approve more emissions via multisig kek. The writing was on the wall for a long time

Now they've deleted the discord server after rugging everyone

62414658
>>62414655
I'm actually amazed they were still running this and never caught any legal flak for the role they played in the terraluna ponzi scheme and collapse

62414664
this one MIGHT be a slurper
hold on

62414718
>>62414664
The shit people will buy instead of Bitcoin. Just lmfao. Sad really.

62414728
so depending on the collat that they start hiking rates on... this might re-peg

62414746
most likely won't re-peg
>reason:
their depreciated cauldrons is where most of the dogshit collat likely is
even if liquidated, they might not cover for a full repeg

62414751
>>62414664

62414752
>>62414655
>manually approve more emissions via multisig
LMAO

62414758
>>62414751
dudes who borrowed from this might be better off letting liquidation happen than getting their dogshit collat back
only God knows what fuckery might be in the "depreciated cauldrons"

62414805
>>62414664
I will not be buying any but if you buy and it repairs, sell it the second it does.
>>62414718
>Buying bitcoin
Lmfao Buy real coins with actual utility.

62414828
>>62414805
I won't buy, this shit has thin liquidity
people might panic and send it to fractions of a cent just for the fuck of it
of course, at that point everyone is going to crowd and buy
but right now, it just looks like something went structurally wrong
and by what that guy posted, considering this shit is managed by fucking multisigs that put rates manually into the contracts...
it kinda does

62414831
>>62414805
>if you buy and it repairs

How’s the weather in Bangladesh?

62414841
>>62414831
Reread what I wrote retard. I said I will not buy it but encouraged him to not baghold the exit repeg if it happens because it never recovers and stays. You are so cooked by doom scrolling reels it is insane. You are incapable of reading a single sentence with patience for comprehension.

62414846
>>62414828
Maybe it will have a bounce somewhere along the line like UST did but do not bag hold it hoping for complete par recovery. Look at the LUNC generals in the archive if you want to feel the blood drain from your face imagining being them (and link is being outperformed by fucking LUNC, if you want to REALLY imagine being a bagholder of the deepest kind lol).

62414884
Why are you retards still in crypto??

62415000
>>62408557
usdc and tether can also depeg, then it's truly over
they can kill crypto at any time they want, wouldn't take more than the epstein class seizing circle and tether or their reserves
their buddies would all sell first, but this is a card they can and will play eventually if the goyim get too powerful and you will lose everything

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62413931
>>62413861
OpenAI needs to dissolve. ITs bankrupt. Their is total dog shit.

62413941
>>62413861
its time to just poop your pants, basically. it's a pants pooping world. that's how it is now. we're all pants poopers. you're a pants pooper or you're out. so, are you in?

62413952
kek I hope your president bails them out and sinks your country like anchor and EU returns to #1

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62413955
>>62413861

62413962

I'm 100% in Micron because I just want to afford a white nurse

62413966
>>62413861

62414024
>>62413861

62414030
>>62413955

62414035

62414070

>>62413962

62414116
>>62414030
>>62413861
The only reason they're postponing it is because of SPCX failure. The market is exhausted, money to invest is running out.

62414118
>>62414070
You sound like a mad little faggot

62414127
>>62414116
So its popping wo infinite dollar printing hyperinflation? Good!

62414138
>>62414118
You sound like a fat, down-bad baggie who limply slinks between corporate metanarratives instead of being a person.

62414147
>>62414116
Yeah..money to invest is running out..definitely not hitting a wall of absurd PE ratios and narrative gush and circular finance round tripping with chips as collateral built on private credit with zero revenue to make good on not measuring up or being able to deliver on the energy supply side of the system build. The market isn't exhausted, the narrative is and the bottom is falling out after fucking years despite the absurd index inclusion and forced passive flow injection of everyone's retirement accounts and pensions into this nothing slop that would be worse for humanity if it were able to deliver.

62414149
>>62413861
Musk successfully pushed them off it seems.

62414181
>>62414138
>>62414147
Bun dem!

62414184
>>62414149
Saaar our gaysex space ai datacenter is not ready yet, ples wait another 200yer with ipo thanks

62414360
>>62414116
What money faggot? all their "money" is directly printed for them lmao

62414388
>>62413861
the capital might flow back into crypto

62414409
Doesn't it keep getting better though?

62414509
>>62414409
no, ai improvement has slowed to a crawl. mythos is a farce, it's barely better than the previous version

62414530
>>62414030
why if his face so puffy?

62414623
>>62414070
both altman and elon are really brokefag pentagon cucks. anthropic's IPO will be the only successful IPO because they told the pentagon and israel to fuck off

62414693
>>62414030
>SpaceX's stock has been volatile
So any other IPO.

item.title: This motherfucker just proved to the entire world beyond any and all doubt, that absolutely NO ONE is trustworthy in cry… — 16 replies — 4.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
This motherfucker just proved to the entire world beyond any and all doubt, that absolutely NO ONE is trustworthy in cry…

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-06-26 05:36 UTC

OP

This motherfucker just proved to the entire world beyond any and all doubt, that absolutely NO ONE is trustworthy in crypto. Not a single goddamn one. ZERO exceptions

Early replies (first 3)

62410760
i mean he looks like a sojack, thats kind of on you

62413165
This, combined with fallout from the LINK AMA, is funny as hell. Assblaster was a fraud

62413241
>>62410743
Give context you nigger

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62413259
>>62413241
Yes, who is the onion and why do I care?>>62413165
>Assblaster was a fraud
This was always obvious

62413262
>>62413241
https://www.coinmetro.com/blog/announcement-onboarding-depositing-and-withdrawals
There are people in their telegram group talking about roping as a result of this. I'm not joking

62413573
>>62413262
So another exchange shutting down and stealing all users funds?

62414199
>>62413262
The ama was hard to watch. The amount of times he had to say he can't guarantee people's funds and that it's up to the courts was irritating. He kept saying they are all in it together and that he was too as in investor, ceo and user of the platform. I bet he didn't halt his own withdrawals though. What a fuckin goon on the whole "reorganization" pitch. Hopefully karma gets that stupid mother fucker. His body language said it all.

62414273
it's over. the sec need to be restored so it can go after every single piece of shit crypto project and exchange

62414281
>>62410743
>what if we had banks but like there we no rules about how the bankers behaved
Truly amazing how anyone thought this wasn't a scam.

62414293
>>62414281
Libertardian dream

62414317
>>62414199
>>62413262
Not your keys, not your crypto. Does remind me of last cycle when Kucoin closed up though. Hmm…

62414659
>>62410743

62414772
imagine trusting a man with a hairline like that? couldnt be me

62414835
>>62410743
My 1 whole btc is on there. I procrastinated putting it my own wallet. Are they scaming or we can get it back?

62414898
How the fuck does an exchange go bankrupt without a huge hack? Its like Trump bankrupting a casino. It should not happen

62414931
>>62410743
tfw a personal friend of mine for years started a crypto coin that actually took off when musk mentioned it a few years ago. it mooned and he sold all he held to pay off his house. black lives matter-tier scammer. you literally cannot trust anyone.
